arguments.task: Research Amazon Selling Partner API using official first-party sources if possible. Open the Amazon SP-API developer docs and capture evidence for: what the API is for; authentication methods and exact auth requirements; whether developer registration/approval is required; any pricing or access tier notes; API types/endpoints supported; rate limit guidance; and any official references to GitHub, MCP servers, or model context protocol. Also browse official pages linked from docs for authorization, usage plans/rate limits, developer registration, and API reference. Return a concise evidence-backed summary with exact source URLs and short supporting quotes or facts from each page.

input.thought: Gather official documentation evidence for Amazon Selling Partner API research.

raw_result.description: Amazon Selling Partner API lets developers programmatically access Amazon seller and vendor data and workflows such as orders, listings, reports, and finances.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe Login with Amazon (LWA) for authorization and AWS IAM for request signing/authentication. Some operations may also require Restricted Data Tokens for sensitive data. Exact per-operation token requirements were not fully verified in the gathered evidence, so review is advised.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official onboarding docs indicate developers must register as a developer and complete onboarding/authorization steps; access depends on seller/vendor authorization and Amazon developer workflow rather than simple anonymous self-serve API keys.

api_surface.breadth: Broad REST API surface with many domain-specific APIs listed in the reference, including Orders, Listings, Reports, Finances, A+ Content, and more.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official evidence of a Model Context Protocol server was found in the gathered official docs. Official GitHub evidence points to API models/SDK-related repositories, not MCP.

raw_result.blocker: Developer onboarding and Amazon authorization/setup requirements add manual gating beyond simple self-serve API access.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Amazon documents usage plans and rate limits for SP-API, generally using per-operation quotas and restore rates; limits can vary by operation and selling-partner context. Exact numeric limits were not fully verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs include dedicated onboarding, authorization, usage-plan/rate-limit guidance, and a large API reference. Amazon also maintains an official GitHub repository for Selling Partner API models, which is a positive ecosystem signal.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some fields remain ambiguous from the already gathered evidence alone: whether the best access-tier classification should be admin_approval_required versus self-serve with seller authorization, whether restricted data token requirements should be explicitly included as an auth method detail, and exact pricing/cost posture plus representative rate-limit numbers were not conclusively verified.
